message:
refactor(abi): rustfmt pass + let-else cleanups + search.h ENTER fix

Mechanical + surgical cleanups across the frankenlibc-abi crate.

search_abi.rs (actual semantic fix)
  * `HashTable::insert` on a hit with `action == Action::ENTER`
    now overwrites the existing entry's `data` pointer, matching
    POSIX hsearch(3): quoting the spec, "If action is ENTER and
    the item is present, the item's data value is set to the new
    value." Previously we returned the existing slot unchanged,
    so callers updating an existing key silently lost the new
    data pointer.

pthread_abi.rs (recursive mutex bookkeeping readability)
  * `pthread_mutex_lock` PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE_TYPE branch
    rewritten with let-else early-returns for the owner/count
    pointer checks, flattening the triple-nested
    `if let Some(_) = …` pyramid. Behavior identical (we still
    bail with EINVAL on NULL owner/count and EAGAIN on lock-count
    overflow) — just more scannable. Same treatment applied at
    the trylock and unlock recursion sites.

Rustfmt pass across the crate
  c11threads_abi.rs, fortify_abi.rs, glibc_internal_abi.rs,
  io_internal_abi.rs, poll_abi.rs, process_abi.rs, resolv_abi.rs,
  socket_abi.rs, stdio_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, time_abi.rs,
  unistd_abi.rs
    * Long `syscall::sys_foo(arg1, arg2, arg3, arg4)` calls that
      used to live on one line inside `match unsafe { … }` are now
      broken across arg-per-line form to match the workspace
      rustfmt config. Many calls to `syscall::sys_name_to_handle_at`,
      `sys_newfstatat`, `sys_accept`, `sys_connect`, `sys_sendmsg`,
      `sys_mmap`, etc. were affected.
    * `use` import blocks re-grouped / re-sorted where they had
      drifted.
    * No semantic change outside search_abi + pthread_abi.

Tests
  * search_abi_test.rs gains targeted coverage for the new ENTER
    overwrite semantics (insert-with-data, lookup sees new data,
    action=FIND does not overwrite). +27 / -5 net.
  * stdlib_abi_test.rs: rustfmt-only.

Related tracker: bd-r2oq (search.h task).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
